# Template_Index — Umbrella/Re-Export Module Template

Use this template for **top-level umbrella modules** (Layer 4) that
aggregate and re-export an entire library sub-hierarchy.
The canonical example in your project is `MyProject.lean` (the root index file).

Umbrella modules serve as **stable public API facades**:
- Callers import ONE file and get everything
- Internal reorganization (splitting, renaming) is hidden from callers
- `assert_not_exists` can guard what should NOT be in scope

## Rules for Index Modules
1. NO proof bodies — only imports and re-exports
2. NO `@[simp]` lemmas — they leak into all importers and may conflict
3. NO `variable` blocks — these do not re-export
4. NO heavy computation — no `#eval` at module top level (slow)
5. YES `export` for selective re-export of namespaces
6. YES `assert_not_exists` to guard accidental heavy dependencies
7. YES `namespace` wrapping for the public API

## References
* [Lean 4 module system](https://lean-lang.org/doc/reference/latest/Modules/)
* MyProject.lean (the actual project index file)

## Tags
template, index, umbrella, re-export, export, namespace, assert_not_exists

```lean
-- TEMPLATE: List ALL sub-module imports here, in DAG layer order.
-- Layer 0 (leaves) first, Layer 4 last.
-- Each import line is a public dependency commitment.

-- ── Layer 0: Foundational ─────────────────────────────────────────────
-- import MyProject.Tactics
-- import MyProject.Foundations
-- import MyProject.Classifiers
-- import MyProject.Profiling

-- ── Layer 1: First-tier Theorems ──────────────────────────────────────
-- import MyProject.Catastrophe
-- import MyProject.Predicates
-- import MyProject.Gaps

-- ── Layer 2: Application Modules ──────────────────────────────────────
-- import MyProject.Pipeline
-- import MyProject.PhasePortrait
-- import MyProject.Stochastic
-- import MyProject.Provenance
-- import MyProject.RL
-- import MyProject.Safety
-- import MyProject.Bypass
-- import MyProject.Feedback

-- ── Layer 3: Cross-Cutting Modules ────────────────────────────────────
-- import MyProject.Stability
-- import MyProject.Contraction

-- NOTE: Layer 4 (this file) imports all of the above.
-- PITFALL: Do NOT import this file from any sub-module — that creates
-- a circular dependency. The umbrella is import-only, never imported by leaves.

-- TEMPLATE: For this template file itself (which has no real sub-modules):
-- We skip the actual imports and illustrate the pattern with comments.

set_option autoImplicit false
```

 ### §1  Namespace Wrapping

```lean
-- TEMPLATE: Wrap the public API in the project namespace.
-- All re-exports become accessible under `MyProject.*`.

namespace MyProject
```

 ### §2  export for Selective Re-Export

```lean
-- TEMPLATE: `export SomeNamespace (someName anotherName)` makes those
-- names accessible as `MyProject.someName` after `open MyProject`.
-- Without `export`, callers must still write `MyProject.Foundations.passG1`.

-- TEMPLATE: Re-export key types so callers don't need sub-namespace:
-- export QualityGates (ContextRecord ExperientialFragment passG1 passG2)
-- export PhaseClassification (CCVPhase classifyCCV)
-- export CCVGating (CCVScore ccvIsValid)
-- export Tactics (nat100_to_real_le_one simplex4_component_le)

-- PITFALL: Do NOT `export` internal helper lemmas or private definitions.
-- Only export the "published API" — the names you commit to keeping stable.

-- PITFALL: Do NOT use `open QualityGates` at top level of the index —
-- it leaks namespace into every downstream importer, causing conflicts.
-- Use `export` (selective) instead of `open` (all).
```

 ### §3  assert_not_exists Guards

```lean
-- TEMPLATE: Use `assert_not_exists` to document what should NOT be in scope.
-- This catches accidental heavy dependencies and serves as documentation.
-- v4.28 FEATURE: `assert_not_exists` and `#check_assertions` (v4.28).

-- TEMPLATE: Guard against accidentally importing native-code-only content:
-- assert_not_exists Lean.Server  -- we don't depend on the LSP server

-- TEMPLATE: Guard against heavy dependencies that would bloat the library:
-- assert_not_exists Mathlib.Geometry.Manifold  -- not needed in your project

-- TEMPLATE: After asserting, use `#check_assertions` to verify all
-- pending assertions are satisfied. v4.28 FEATURE.
-- NOTE: In heavily-imported files, `#check_assertions` may produce a long
-- list of assertions from transitive imports. Best used in leaf modules or
-- after `assert_not_exists` guards.
-- #check_assertions
```

 ### §4  Version Documentation

```lean
-- TEMPLATE: Document the exact versions this umbrella was validated against.
-- This helps future maintainers know what to update.

/-- your project library version constraints.
    - Lean 4: v4.28.0
    - Mathlib: v4.28.0 (`leanprover-community/mathlib4` @ `v4.28.0`)
    - cslib: `11b70e5` (pinned commit)

    Update this docstring whenever `lake-manifest.json` changes. -/
def projectVersionNote : String :=
  "MyProject validated on Lean v4.28.0 + Mathlib v4.28.0 + cslib@11b70e5"
```

 ### §5  What NOT to Put in an Index Module

```lean
-- TEMPLATE: The following patterns are FORBIDDEN in index modules:

-- ❌ FORBIDDEN: Proof bodies
-- theorem myProof : 1 + 1 = 2 := by grind
-- (Put proofs in appropriate sub-modules)

-- ❌ FORBIDDEN: @[simp] lemmas
-- @[simp] theorem simp_thing : foo = bar := ...
-- (Would attach to every importer's simp set — unpredictable)

-- ❌ FORBIDDEN: variable blocks
-- variable (n : Nat)
-- (Variables are not re-exported; this adds noise)

-- ❌ FORBIDDEN: Heavy computation at module top
-- #eval expensiveComputation 1000
-- (Evaluated on every import)

-- ❌ FORBIDDEN: open at module top
-- open QualityGates
-- (Leaks namespace into all importers)

-- ✅ ALLOWED: Import statements
-- ✅ ALLOWED: export (selective name re-export)
-- ✅ ALLOWED: assert_not_exists guards
-- ✅ ALLOWED: namespace declarations
-- ✅ ALLOWED: /-- -/ module docstrings
-- ✅ ALLOWED: def for version notes (non-computational, or tiny)
```

 ### §6  #import_path for Debugging Import Chains

```lean
-- TEMPLATE: During development, use `#import_path` to trace how a name
-- gets imported. v4.28 FEATURE.
-- NOTE: `#import_path` takes a DECLARATION NAME (not a module name).
-- #import_path MyProject.Predicates.passG1  -- shows the import chain to this decl

-- TEMPLATE: If `#import_path` reveals an unexpected transitive import,
-- add `assert_not_exists` to guard it.
```

 ### §7  lake shake for Unused Imports

```lean
-- TEMPLATE: After the umbrella module stabilizes, run:
--   lake shake
-- to identify sub-modules that are imported but contribute nothing
-- to the public API. Remove those imports.
-- v4.28 FEATURE: `lake shake` is available in Lean v4.28.

-- PITFALL: `lake shake` may suggest removing imports that are kept
-- for their SIDE EFFECTS (e.g., @[simp] lemmas added to global sets).
-- Review each suggestion before removing.
```

 ### §8  Umbrella Module Checklist

Before committing:
- [ ] Imports in DAG layer order (leaf → umbrella)
- [ ] `export` used for public API names (not `open`)
- [ ] `assert_not_exists` for all known unwanted dependencies
- [ ] `#check_assertions` verifies all assertions (v4.28)
- [ ] NO proof bodies in this file
- [ ] NO `@[simp]` lemmas in this file
- [ ] NO bare `open` at module top
- [ ] Version note `def` updated to match `lake-manifest.json`
- [ ] `lake shake` run to clean unused imports
- [ ] All sub-module imports verified with `lake build`

```lean
end MyProject
```
